name: Aaron Jones
headline: Aging dual-threat slips into committee, flex value only
body:
The 2025 tape flags real decline: Jones managed just 12 games, a -0.057 EPA per play, and a 4.15 yards-per-carry mark that trailed his 4.46 in a 17-game 2024 when he finished RB7 by our rank. His explosive-run rate dipped to 9.09 percent, breakaway runs to 3.79, and he forced a broken tackle only once every 26.4 carries while gaining a thin 1.77 yards after contact. The 90.7 fantasy points and rank of 39 reflect a back past his ground-game prime.

The receiving role is what keeps him relevant. He still ran 198 routes on a 55.1 percent snap share and turned 41 targets into 28 catches, banking 227 yards after the catch and an 8.8 percent target share. Now 30 in Minnesota, he splits early downs with Jordan Mason while owning passing-down work, leaving him a PPR-leaning flex with a capped ceiling. Hamstring, hip, and shoulder issues make a full season unlikely, so treat him as a late-round dart, not a weekly starter.
